name: Sopa De Grao
recipeIngredient: 3 tbsp Peanut, corn or vegetable oil
recipeInstructions:
1. SOAK THE GARBANZOS overnight in 6 cups of the water in a large heavy kettle.
2. The next day, add the remaining 2 cups water, bring to a gentle boil, cover, and cook for 4-to-5 hours or until the garbanzos are very tender.
3. Meanwhile, stir-fry the garlic and onions in the peanut oil in a second large, heavy kettle over moderate heat for 12-to-15 minutes until lightly browned.
4. Add the potatoes and stir-fry for 2-to-3 minutes; add the thyme, coriander, and bay leaf, turn the heat down to low, and allow to mellow about 10 minutes.
5. Pour in the broth, bring all to a gentle simmer, cover, and cook slowly.
6. As soon as you've set the 2 kettles to simmer, stir-fry the pepperoni in a large heavy skillet for 3-to-5 minutes--just until lightly browned and most of the drippings have cooked out.
7. Drain the pepperoni well, reserving 1 tablespoon of the drippings. Set aside.
8. Return 1 tablespoon drippings to the skillet, add the spinach and stir-fry for 2-to-3 minutes over moderate heat just until glazed and intensely green; reserve.
9. When the garbanzos are tender, puree 1/2 of them by buzzing in a food processor for 60 seconds, adding only enough kettle liquid to puree easily.
10. Return the pureed garbanzos to the kettle in which they cooked.
11. Now puree the potato mixture in 2 batches, buzzing each for 30-to-40 seconds in a food processor.
12. Blend the potato mixture into the garbanzo mixture, add the reserved pepperoni and spinach, cover, and simmer for 30-to-40 minutes--just long enough to mellow the flavors.
13. Season to taste with salt and pepper, remove the bay leaf, then drizzle with olive oil.
14. Makes 6 to 8 Servings
